idioms.name: use_try_with_resources
idioms.description: Use try-with-resources for all AutoCloseable objects — streams, connections, readers, writers.
idioms.detection: Detect AutoCloseable objects created without try-with-resources. Detect manual close() in finally blocks.
idioms.bad_example:
InputStream is = new FileInputStream(f);
try { ... } finally { is.close(); }

idioms.name: use_optional_properly
idioms.description: Use Optional for return types that may be absent. Never use Optional as a field or method parameter.
idioms.detection: Detect methods returning null where Optional would be appropriate. Detect Optional used as field type or parameter.
idioms.fix: Return Optional.ofNullable() instead of null. Use orElse/orElseGet/map/flatMap to unwrap.
idioms.bad_example: public User findUser(int id) { ... return null; }
idioms.good_example: public Optional<User> findUser(int id) { ... return Optional.ofNullable(user); }

idioms.name: use_records
idioms.description: Use Java records (16+) for immutable data carriers instead of classes with boilerplate getters, equals, hashCode.
idioms.detection: Detect classes with only final fields, getters, equals/hashCode, toString, and no behaviour.
idioms.bad_example: public class Point { private final int x; private final int y; public Point(int x, int y) { this.x = x; this.y = y; } ... }
idioms.good_example: public record Point(int x, int y) {}

idioms.name: use_stream_api
idioms.description: Use the Stream API for collection transformations instead of imperative loops with temporary lists.
idioms.detection: Detect for loops building new collections via add() where stream map/filter/collect would work.
idioms.fix: Replace with stream().map().filter().collect(). Use Collectors.toList() or toUnmodifiableList().
idioms.bad_example:
List<String> names = new ArrayList<>();
for (User u : users) { names.add(u.getName()); }

idioms.name: use_switch_expressions
idioms.description: Use switch expressions (14+) with arrow syntax and exhaustiveness checking instead of traditional switch statements.
idioms.detection: Detect traditional switch with break statements that could be switch expressions.
idioms.fix: Convert to switch expression with -> syntax. Use sealed types for exhaustive matching.
idioms.bad_example: switch (day) { case MON: result = 1; break; case TUE: result = 2; break; default: result = 0; }
idioms.good_example: var result = switch (day) { case MON -> 1; case TUE -> 2; default -> 0; };

idioms.name: no_checked_exception_abuse
idioms.description: Don't declare checked exceptions on every method. Use unchecked exceptions for programming errors, checked only for recoverable conditions.
idioms.detection: Detect methods throwing Exception or multiple unrelated checked exceptions. Detect catch blocks that just wrap and rethrow.
idioms.fix: Use unchecked exceptions (IllegalArgumentException, IllegalStateException) for bugs. Reserve checked exceptions for genuinely recoverable situations.
idioms.bad_example: public void process(String s) throws Exception { ... }
idioms.good_example: public void process(String s) { if (s == null) throw new IllegalArgumentException("s must not be null"); }
